name: marketsui-platform
description: MarketsUI platform monorepo — config-driven UI framework for capital markets trading apps on OpenFin. Consolidates @grid-customizer, markets-ui, fi-trading-terminal, and star.

scripts.dev:openfin: concurrently -k -n vite,openfin -c cyan,magenta "npm run dev -w @starui/demo-react -- --no-open" "wait-on http://localhost:5190 && node tools/scripts/launch-openfin.mjs http://localhost:5190/openfin/manifest.json"
scripts.dev:openfin:markets-react: concurrently -k -n vite,openfin -c cyan,magenta "npm run dev -w @starui/markets-ui-react-reference -- --no-open" "wait-on http://localhost:5174 && node tools/scripts/launch-openfin.mjs http://localhost:5174/platform/manifest.fin.json"
scripts.clean: rimraf -g node_modules apps/*/node_modules packages/*/*/node_modules apps/*/dist packages/*/*/dist .turbo

//optionalDependencies-note: Workaround for npm 10.x bug (https://github.com/npm/cli/issues/4828) — when package-lock.json is generated on one platform, `npm ci` on a different platform skips installing rollup's platform-specific native binaries from vite/rollup's nested optionalDependencies. Declaring them explicitly here forces npm ci to install whichever one matches the current platform (each binary's package.json carries its own `os`/`cpu` constraints, so only one actually installs). Without this, CI fails with 'Cannot find module @rollup/rollup-linux-x64-gnu'.
